description:
The CI server that builds the company’s Android application writes one text log for every day under  
  /home/user/mobile_ci/logs/.  
  •  Each daily file is named build_YYYY-MM-DD.log  
  •  Inside every file there are one-or-more *build sections*, each delimited exactly by the two marker
     lines “=== BUILD START ===” and “=== BUILD END ===” (three = symbols, one blank on each side).

  Every section always contains the following five (and only these five) key/value lines, in this
  fixed order:

      JobID: <integer>
      Variant: <debug|release>
      Timestamp: <ISO-8601 timestamp, always ends with “Z”>
      Status: <SUCCESS|FAILURE>
      Reason: <free-text reason – present **only when Status is FAILURE**>

  As the new mobile build engineer you must generate two artefacts that downstream analytics jobs rely
  on:

  1.  A CSV summary of all failed builds
      Path: /home/user/mobile_ci/reports/failed_builds_summary.csv  
      Exact layout requirements:  
      •  The file **must start with** the header line  
        job_id,variant,timestamp,reason  
      •  One line per failed build follows, ordered by the Timestamp field in ascending
         chronological order.  
      •  Fields are separated by the single character “,” (comma).  
      •  Do **NOT** surround the field values with quotes unless the value itself contains a comma,
         in which case wrap that single value in double quotes `"` (RFC 4180 style). In today’s
         sample data none of the provided reasons contain commas, so no quotes are expected.  
      •  The finished file must therefore contain exactly four data lines for today’s sample data,
         looking like this (header plus three rows):

           job_id,variant,timestamp,reason
           1002,release,2024-06-01T11:00:00Z,Unit tests failed
           1003,debug,2024-06-02T09:05:00Z,Gradle compilation error
           1005,release,2024-06-03T08:40:00Z,Lint violations

      Create the containing directory /home/user/mobile_ci/reports if it does not exist.

  2.  A small processing log
      Path: /home/user/mobile_ci/reports/processing.log  
      Exact layout requirements (three lines, nothing else, final newline optional):  

          FILES_PROCESSED: <integer>
          FAILURES_FOUND: <integer>
          REPORT_GENERATED_AT: STATIC_TEST_TIMESTAMP

      •  <integer> placeholders above are decimal counts that you compute at runtime.  
         – FILES_PROCESSED is the number of build_*.log files you actually inspected.  
         – FAILURES_FOUND is the number of failed build sections that were written to the CSV.  
      •  For reproducibility the timestamp **must literally be the constant string
        “STATIC_TEST_TIMESTAMP” (without quotes).**

  Hints & boundary conditions that the automated grader will rely on:

  •  All daily log files available at task start reside directly under
     /home/user/mobile_ci/logs/ and their names all match the glob build_*.log.  
  •  Only those three files need to be parsed; no sub-directories will exist.  
  •  You do not need root privileges; your user already has write permission to the
     /home/user/mobile_ci/ directory tree.  
  •  The grader will verify that:
       – The CSV header is present and spelled exactly as specified;  
       – The three expected data lines are present **in ascending timestamp order**;  
       – processing.log contains exactly three newline-separated lines whose prefixes and numeric
         values match the specification, and whose third line equals
         “REPORT_GENERATED_AT: STATIC_TEST_TIMESTAMP”.  
  •  Use any POSIX-compatible shell tools you like (grep, awk, sed, cut, sort, printf, etc.) but do
     not hard-code paths other than those explicitly given above.

  Goal: when the grader opens a fresh terminal in the working directory and prints the two files,
  they must match the precise formats described here.
truth:
Initial file system state that the automated tests expect before the agent starts:

  1. Directory structure (all directories already exist and are writable by user):
     /home/user/mobile_ci/
     /home/user/mobile_ci/logs/

  2. Exact contents of the three daily log files that are present at task start:

     File: /home/user/mobile_ci/logs/build_2024-06-01.log
     ----------------------------------------------------------------
     === BUILD START ===
     JobID: 1001
     Variant: debug
     Timestamp: 2024-06-01T10:15:00Z
     Status: SUCCESS
     === BUILD END ===

     === BUILD START ===
     JobID: 1002
     Variant: release
     Timestamp: 2024-06-01T11:00:00Z
     Status: FAILURE
     Reason: Unit tests failed
     === BUILD END ===
     ----------------------------------------------------------------

     File: /home/user/mobile_ci/logs/build_2024-06-02.log
     ----------------------------------------------------------------
     === BUILD START ===
     JobID: 1003
     Variant: debug
     Timestamp: 2024-06-02T09:05:00Z
     Status: FAILURE
     Reason: Gradle compilation error
     === BUILD END ===

     === BUILD START ===
     JobID: 1004
     Variant: release
     Timestamp: 2024-06-02T14:20:00Z
     Status: SUCCESS
     === BUILD END ===
     ----------------------------------------------------------------

     File: /home/user/mobile_ci/logs/build_2024-06-03.log
     ----------------------------------------------------------------
     === BUILD START ===
     JobID: 1005
     Variant: release
     Timestamp: 2024-06-03T08:40:00Z
     Status: FAILURE
     Reason: Lint violations
     === BUILD END ===
     ----------------------------------------------------------------

  3. Expected artefacts **after** the agent completes the task:

     a) /home/user/mobile_ci/reports/failed_builds_summary.csv  
        (exactly five lines, UNIX LF line endings):

        job_id,variant,timestamp,reason
        1002,release,2024-06-01T11:00:00Z,Unit tests failed
        1003,debug,2024-06-02T09:05:00Z,Gradle compilation error
        1005,release,2024-06-03T08:40:00Z,Lint violations

     b) /home/user/mobile_ci/reports/processing.log  
        (three lines, UNIX LF line endings):

        FILES_PROCESSED: 3
        FAILURES_FOUND: 3
        REPORT_GENERATED_AT: STATIC_TEST_TIMESTAMP
